title: Find Relief from Over Bleeding During Periods with These 8 Herbal Decoctions

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Ligustilide is good for over bleeding during periods because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms associated with excessive uterine contractions.

Coumarins are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce menstrual flow and ease cramping, thereby providing relief from heavy menstrual bleeding.
Butylphthalide is good for over bleeding during periods because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce blood loss and ease menstrual cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis decoctions, also known as Dong Quai, is a traditional Chinese herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to treat menstrual problems. It's believed to be beneficial for heavy and prolonged menstrual bleeding due to its rich content of iron and other essential minerals. These minerals help to replace the lost blood and reduce the risk of anemia. The decoction is also thought to have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal system, which can help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce excessive bleeding. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of Angelica sinensis may help to ease cramps and discomfort associated with heavy bleeding. Some studies suggest that the herbal remedy may also have a positive effect on the blood's ability to clot, which can help to stop excessive bleeding. The combination of these properties makes Angelica sinensis decoctions a popular natural remedy for women experiencing over bleeding during their periods.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Althaea officinalis herbal decoctions that help with over bleeding during periods, you will need dried roots of the marshmallow plant, a saucepan, water, and a clean container for storage. Start by measuring 1-2 teaspoons of dried Althaea officinalis roots for every 1 cup of water. Combine the roots and water in the saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties. After the simmering time, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the liquid to cool down. Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, and discard the solids. You can drink 1-2 cups of this decoction 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional, to help reduce excessive bleeding during periods. It is essential to note that Althaea officinalis is not a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with other treatments under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce menstrual blood loss and ease cramps, which can help to regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce excessive bleeding.
Iridoids help to stop over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the uterus, promoting faster healing and decreasing blood loss.
Saponins are good for over bleeding during periods because they help to reduce inflammation and strengthen blood vessels, thus controlling heavy menstrual flow and promoting healthy uterine function.

Agnuside helps to reduce heavy bleeding during periods by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce uterine contractions and improve blood vessel function, leading to a decrease in menstrual blood flow.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus decoctions, also known as chasteberry, have been used for centuries to help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding during periods. The active compounds in this herb, such as flavonoids and iridoids, may help to balance hormonal levels in the body, particularly estrogen and progesterone. When estrogen and progesterone are in balance, menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and mood swings often decrease. By reducing inflammation and improving the body's ability to absorb and use iron, Vitex agnus-castus decoctions may also help to alleviate anemia, a common condition associated with heavy menstrual bleeding. Additionally, this herb may help to ease uterine cramps, making periods more manageable for women with fibroids or endometriosis. When taken as a decoction, Vitex agnus-castus can be easily absorbed into the body, allowing its benefits to take effect quickly.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Althaea officinalis herbal decoction that helps with over bleeding during periods, start by gathering 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root. Combine the dried root with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan, and then reduce the heat to low. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the root to release its medicinal properties into the water. Next, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the mixture to cool down. Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the solids, and enjoy the decoction warm or at room temperature. You can repeat this process 2-3 times a day to help regulate your menstrual flow and alleviate excessive bleeding. It's essential to note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're experiencing severe or prolonged bleeding.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for over bleeding during periods because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease menstrual cramps, thereby reducing excessive bleeding.
Flavonoids help to reduce over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the uterus, thus reducing excessive bleeding.

Polyphenols are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce blood loss and ease menstrual cramps by reducing prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to heavy bleeding.

Saponins are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding by relaxing the uterine muscles and improving blood flow.
Triterpenoids are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing heavy and prolonged bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has been used for centuries to help with menstrual problems. The decoctions made from this root are believed to be beneficial for women who experience heavy or irregular bleeding during their periods. The root is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the uterine lining, reducing the risk of heavy bleeding. It may also help to balance the body's hormonal levels, which can lead to a more regular menstrual cycle. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been shown to have antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve cramps and discomfort associated with menstrual periods. Some research suggests that the root may also have a positive effect on the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause the uterus to contract and lead to heavy bleeding. By reducing the production of these substances, Glycyrrhiza glabra may help to alleviate the symptoms of over-bleeding during periods.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Althaea officinalis herbal decoction to help with over bleeding during periods, start by gathering dried roots of the plant, also known as marshmallow root. First, rinse a tablespoon of the dried root in a small bowl of water, then place it in a pot of boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the root steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing its medicinal properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, turn off the heat and let the decoction sit for 5-10 minutes, allowing it to cool down a bit. Once cooled, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding the solids. The resulting decoction can be consumed 2-3 times a day, 1-2 tablespoons at a time, to help reduce excessive menstrual bleeding and alleviate cramps. It's essential to note that Althaea officinalis is not a substitute for medical treatment and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Phenolic acids are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce blood loss and ease menstrual cramps by soothing the uterus and improving blood vessel health.

Isoferulic acid is good for over bleeding during periods because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce blood loss and promote healthy uterine contractions, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with heavy menstrual flow.
Ferulic acid helps to stop over bleeding during periods because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce blood loss and promote healing in the uterus lining.
Lignans are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding by strengthening blood vessel walls.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa decoctions, also known as black cohosh, is a herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to help with menstrual issues. It's particularly useful for women who experience heavy or prolonged bleeding during their periods. This is because black cohosh contains compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the uterus, which can help to slow down bleeding. Additionally, it can also help to regulate the hormonal balance in the body, which is often disrupted during menstruation, leading to heavy bleeding. Black cohosh decoctions can also help to ease cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS, such as mood swings and fatigue. When taken as a decoction, the active compounds in black cohosh are released and absorbed more easily into the body, allowing for faster relief from symptoms. Many women have found that taking a black cohosh decoction regularly can help to reduce the severity and frequency of heavy bleeding, making it a popular natural remedy for menstrual issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Althaea officinalis herbal decoction that helps with over bleeding during periods, start by gathering dried root of the marshmallow plant, which is another name for Althaea officinalis. Take a teaspoon of the dried root and add it to a cup of boiling water. Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water. Strain the mixture into another cup to remove the root, and discard the solids. You can add a little honey or sugar to taste, if needed, and drink the warm decoction 2-3 times a day. It's recommended to drink it after meals and before bedtime to help reduce menstrual flow. You can also add a few drops of vitamin E oil or aloe vera gel to the decoction for added benefits. Note that Althaea officinalis is not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult with a healthcare professional if your menstrual bleeding is heavy or prolonged.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for over bleeding during periods because it helps to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which in turn reduces the flow of blood during menstrual cycles.

Kaempferol is good for over bleeding during periods because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce menstrual cramps and bleeding by improving blood vessel function and reducing oxidative stress.
Tannins are good for over bleeding during periods because they have astringent properties that help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and promoting clotting.
Polyphenols are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce blood loss and soothe the uterus, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle.

Isoflavones can help with over bleeding during periods because they have a balancing effect on hormone levels, particularly estrogen, which can help to regulate menstrual flow and reduce excessive bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense decoctions, also known as red clover, are used to help with heavy bleeding during periods. The plant has been studied for its ability to reduce the symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS) and heavy menstrual bleeding. The isoflavones present in red clover, such as formononetin and biochanin A, may help to reduce bleeding by acting as a natural estrogen. This can help to regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce the severity of heavy bleeding. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of red clover may also help to reduce bloating and cramps associated with PMS. The decoction can be made by steeping the dried flowers in hot water, and can be consumed as a tea. Some studies have shown that red clover extract can reduce menstrual blood loss by up to 50%, making it a potentially useful natural remedy for women experiencing heavy bleeding during their periods.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Althaea officinalis herbal decoction for over bleeding during periods, you will need dried marshmallow root. Start by boiling a liter of water in a saucepan. Then, add two to three teaspoons of dried marshmallow root to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly. After it has cooled down, you can strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for later use. To use the decoction, you can drink one to two cups of it throughout the day, especially during your period. It's recommended to take a warm bath with the decoction as a final rinse for added relief. Repeat this process as needed to help reduce heavy bleeding and soothe menstrual cramps.

Tannins are good for over bleeding during periods because they help to reduce inflammation and slow down bleeding in the uterus by acting as a natural astringent, which reduces blood loss and promotes healing.
Flavonoids help to reduce over bleeding during periods by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce blood clotting and promote healthy blood flow.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora decoctions are made from the root of the Peony plant. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to help with heavy menstrual bleeding. The root contains compounds that help to stop bleeding and calm the uterus, making it a popular remedy for women who experience over bleeding during their periods. When taken as a decoction, the compounds in Paeonia lactiflora are absorbed by the body and can help to reduce the flow of blood during heavy periods. This can provide relief from discomfort, pain, and fatigue that often come with over bleeding. Some research suggests that Paeonia lactiflora may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of heavy periods. However, it is essential to note that Paeonia lactiflora decoctions should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment, but rather as a complementary therapy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal decoction from Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow root, which can help with heavy bleeding during periods, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried root. You can dry the root yourself by tying it in a paper bag and placing it in a warm, dark place for a few days, or purchase it from a health food store. Next, add the root to 2 cups of boiling water, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 10-15 minutes. The longer it cooks, the stronger the decoction will be. Once the time has passed, remove the root from the water and let the decoction cool. You can strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the root. The decoction can be taken as needed, 2-3 times a day, in 1/2 cup amounts, sweetened with honey if desired. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are pregnant.

Kaempferol is good for over bleeding during periods because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce menstrual flow and ease pain and discomfort.

Ginkgolide b is good for over bleeding during periods because it helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood clotting by inhibiting the formation of bradykinin, a substance that can lead to excessive bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoction is a herbal remedy that can help with heavy menstrual bleeding. It contains compounds that help to thin the blood and improve blood flow, which can reduce bleeding. The decoction also contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and discomfort in the uterus. This can help to ease cramps and other symptoms associated with heavy periods. The ginkgo biloba tree has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its extracts have been shown to have a positive effect on menstrual health. The decoction is also believed to help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to heavy bleeding. Some women find that taking ginkgo biloba decoction helps to reduce the frequency and severity of their periods, and can even help to alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and fatigue that often accompany heavy bleeding.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Althaea officinalis herbal decoction that can help with over bleeding during periods, start by gathering 2-3 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, which is also known as Althaea officinalis. Rinse the dried herb with cold water to remove any impurities. Then, add the rinsed marshmallow root to a medium-sized pot and pour 2 cups of boiling water over it. Reduce the heat to a low setting and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herb to release its medicinal properties into the water. After the decoction has cooled slightly, strain it into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herb. Discard the solids and drink the decoction 2-3 times a day to help alleviate excessive bleeding during periods. It's essential to note that you should consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are pregnant.

Flavonoids help reduce over bleeding during periods by acting as an astringent that helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the uterus, thereby reducing heavy menstrual bleeding.

Polyphenols are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and protect the body from damage, which can help to stop or reduce heavy menstrual bleeding.

Saponins are good for over bleeding during periods because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding by soothing the uterus and regulating menstrual flow.
Mucilages help to reduce heavy bleeding during periods by forming a protective barrier on the lining of the uterus, soothing and calming the blood vessels to prevent excessive bleeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis decoctions are made from the roots of the marshmallow plant. They have been used for centuries to help with heavy and painful menstrual bleeding. The roots contain a type of fiber called mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the uterus. When the uterus is calm, it contracts less and bleeding slows down. The mucilage also helps to protect the lining of the uterus and the cervix, reducing the risk of bleeding and cramping. Additionally, the decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort during heavy periods. Drinking Althaea officinalis decoctions may also help to regulate menstrual cycles and improve overall uterine health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Althaea officinalis herbal decoction to help with over bleeding during periods, start by gathering dried marshmallow roots, which are the parts of the plant used to make the decoction. Next, measure out one teaspoon of dried roots for every cup of water you plan to use. Then, boil the water and add the measured roots to the water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. This will allow the roots' medicinal properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time has passed, remove the roots from the water and let the decoction cool. Once cooled, you can drink the decoction as is, or mix it with other herbal teas or warm water to make it more palatable. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the decoction per day, ideally before or during your period, to help regulate bleeding and alleviate discomfort.

intro_desc: During menstrual periods, some women experience heavy bleeding, which can be painful and disrupt daily life. This can be due to hormonal imbalances, stress, or other factors. Herbal decoctions can provide relief by addressing the underlying causes. Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, is a traditional Chinese herb that helps regulate menstrual cycles and reduces excessive bleeding. It contains compounds that improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate heavy bleeding.  Vitex agnus-castus, or chaste tree, is another herb that helps balance hormones, particularly estrogen and progesterone. When these hormones are in balance, menstrual cycles become more regular and less severe. Vitex agnus-castus also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe the uterus and reduce bleeding. By using herbal decoctions containing these herbs, women can reduce the severity of their periods and improve their overall well-being.  It's essential to address heavy bleeding during periods because it can lead to iron deficiency, fatigue, and other health issues. Iron deficiency, in particular, can cause anemia, which can impair cognitive function, increase the risk of infections, and worsen menstrual cramps. By regulating menstrual bleeding, women can prevent these complications and enjoy better physical and mental health. This, in turn, can improve their daily lives, allowing them to work, exercise, and engage in activities without being held back by heavy bleeding. By using herbal decoctions containing Angelica sinensis and Vitex agnus-castus, women can take a natural and effective approach to addressing this issue and enjoying a healthier, more active life.
supplementary_best: A combination of Angelica sinensis, Cimicifuga racemosa, and Paeonia lactiflora can be effective in helping to regulate and reduce heavy bleeding during periods.  Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, is a traditional Chinese medicine that has been used for centuries to balance and regulate menstrual cycles. It has been shown to have a positive effect on blood flow and is rich in iron, which is essential for preventing anemia.  Cimicifuga racemosa, or black cohosh, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce cramping and other symptoms associated with heavy bleeding. It also has a natural ability to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to heavy or irregular periods.  Paeonia lactiflora, or peony root, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its ability to calm and balance the body's energy. It has a soothing effect on the uterus and can help to reduce heavy bleeding by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.  Together, these three herbs work synergistically to regulate menstrual cycles, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making them a well-rounded combination for addressing heavy bleeding during periods.
supplementary_avoid: If you have heavy bleeding during your periods, there are some herbal decoctions you should avoid. One such herb is 'Rheum palmatum', also known as rhubarb root. This herb can cause the uterus to contract and reduce blood flow, but it can also stimulate the uterus to bleed more if it's already weak. Consuming rhubarb root in large amounts or for a long time can lead to more bleeding.  Another herb to be cautious with is 'Capsella bursa-pastoris', or shepherd's purse. This herb has been used to reduce menstrual bleeding, but it can also increase the risk of bleeding if taken in excess. The reason is that shepherd's purse can stimulate the uterus to contract and release blood.  You should also avoid 'Pulsatilla vulgaris', or pasque flower. While pasque flower can help with menstrual cramps, it can also stimulate the uterus to bleed more if it's already weak. This is because pasque flower contains compounds that can cause the uterus to contract.  Lastly, avoid 'Taxus baccata', or yew. Yew contains taxine, a toxic compound that can cause the uterus to contract and lead to more bleeding. While yew has been used in traditional medicine, it's not suitable for people with heavy bleeding during periods.  These herbs should be used with caution or avoided altogether if you have heavy bleeding during your periods. It's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
supplementary_other_remedies: For women who experience heavy bleeding during their periods, there are several natural remedies beyond herbal decoctions that can be helpful. Cold compresses or an ice pack on the lower abdomen can be applied to help reduce bleeding by constricting the blood vessels and slowing down the flow of blood. This is based on the principle of the body's natural response to cold temperatures, which causes the blood vessels to narrow and reduce blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, some women find relief by increasing their physical activity, such as taking a brisk walk, which can stimulate the uterus to contract and reduce bleeding. Other natural remedies include applying heat to the lower abdomen, which can help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce bleeding. This is because heat can cause the blood vessels to dilate, allowing the blood to flow more freely and potentially reducing the need for the uterus to contract as much to stop the bleeding.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding may also have other health issues. One common problem is anemia, a condition where the body doesn't have enough red blood cells to carry oxygen. This is often due to iron deficiency, which can be treated with herbs like dandelion root, red clover, and nettle leaf. These plants are rich in iron and can help replenish the body's stores.  Another issue is fatigue, which can be caused by low iron levels and other nutritional deficiencies. Ginseng, ashwagandha, and licorice root are adaptogenic herbs that can help reduce fatigue by balancing hormone levels and improving energy production.  Some individuals with heavy menstrual bleeding may also experience digestive issues like constipation or diarrhea. This can be due to changes in hormone levels, which can affect gut function. Herbs like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile can help soothe digestive issues by relaxing the muscles and reducing inflammation.  Heavy menstrual bleeding can also be linked to hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen dominance. Herbs like vitex, saw palmetto, and spearmint can help regulate hormone levels by inhibiting the production of estrogen and promoting the production of progesterone.  Lastly, some women with heavy menstrual bleeding may experience mood changes, such as anxiety, depression, and irritability. This can be caused by hormonal fluctuations and nutritional deficiencies. Herbs like St. John's Wort, passionflower, and valerian root can help alleviate mood symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
